name: Veritas Investments
notes: SF's largest residential landlord. ~250+ buildings. Facing foreclosure on 66 buildings ($551M debt). Sold 76-building portfolio to PCCP LLC for $540M. Sold 20 buildings to Prado Group ($124M). Marketed furnished units via RentSFNow to evade rent control. Actively downsizing/restructuring.

name: Mosser Companies
notes: 61 SF buildings. 14 buildings placed in receivership (2025) — 428 units at risk. Defaulted on $88M loan. Family succession battle. JP Morgan Chase seeking foreclosure on 13 properties. Distressed and actively losing properties to lenders.

name: Equity Residential
notes: Publicly traded REIT (EQR). Chicago-based institutional owner. 23 properties in SF. Top apartment owner by unit count. Primarily newer/luxury stock. Stable institutional owner.

name: Essex Property Trust
notes: Publicly traded REIT (ESS). Founded by George Marcus. 14 properties in SF. Notable properties include Mosso (463 units, LEED Gold) and Hillsdale Garden (697 units). Stable institutional owner.

name: Maximus Real Estate Partners
notes: 4 properties in SF including Parkmerced (3,221 units on 144 acres, purchased 2014 for $1.4B). Plans for $6B 20-year transformation. Has faced eviction actions at Parkmerced.

name: Lembi Group
notes: At peak: 307 buildings, 6,500+ units. Sued by SF City Attorney (2006) for intimidation, armed guards, utility shutoffs, retaliatory evictions, illegal construction. Filed Chapter 11 (Feb 2020). Lost 51+ properties to foreclosure. Many former properties now controlled by Veritas. Largely defunct/restructured.

name: Related Companies
notes: NYC-based firm, $30B+ in assets globally. 4,222 SF multifamily units (862 fully affordable). Active institutional owner.

name: Trinity Properties
notes: 3,100+ units, 13 communities in SF. Notable properties: 33 8th Street at Trinity Place (540 units, under construction), 1000 Chestnut. Surveillance concerns at 1177 Market Street using extensive camera network via G4S.

name: Prado Group
notes: Connected to Prometheus Real Estate. 80+ properties developed since 2003, $1.38B value. Recently absorbed 20 buildings (316 units) from Veritas for $124M. History of evictions.

name: Prometheus Real Estate Group
notes: Largest private multifamily owner in the Bay Area. 13,000+ apartments Bay Area-wide (SF, Seattle, Portland). $2B company. Based in San Mateo. Diller family.

name: Flynn Development Group
notes: 3,000+ residential and commercial units Bay Area-wide. History of evictions to push rents, manipulation of Costa-Hawkins Act. Spent ~$870K to defeat tenant-protective Proposition 10.

name: Robert Imhoff / Landmark Realty
notes: Hundreds of properties across SF + Kansas City. Ellis Act evictions at 3301-3311 Cesar Chavez (12 units, targeting Latino families + 1969 tenant). Buyout/Ellis at 1136 Guerrero (5 units). 144 Airbnb listings. Fire code violations at 641 O'Farrell (21+ since 1989).

name: Sergio Iantorno
notes: ~70 properties in SF. Notorious for TIC conversions via OMI/Ellis evictions, Capital Improvement evictions. Harassment tactics. Attempted eviction of artist Rene Yanez from 35-year Mission home. Sold 5 buildings to SF Community Land Trust / MEDA through Small Sites Program ($8.15M).

name: Sophie & Jeffrey Lau
notes: 10 buildings, ~70 units. 460+ DBI complaints. 100+ lawsuits (1986-2023). Sued tenants ~60 times. Chronic issues: leaking ceilings, broken appliances, mold, no heat. Raised tenant rent from $1,800 to $8,000. Failed to pay vacancy tax. Called SF's most hated landlord (SF Standard, March 2025).

name: Anne Kihagi
notes: Dubbed SF's cruelest landlord. $2.4M+ fine from City Attorney (2017). $7M+ total liability. All properties placed in receivership. Jailed in West Hollywood for Ellis Act violations. Tactics: utility shutoffs, armed guards, surveillance cameras, fraudulent OMI evictions, targeting elderly/disabled tenants. No longer a SF landlord.

name: Kaushik Dattani / Dattani & Company
notes: Properties in Mission District 22nd Street corridor, Capp Street, Valencia. Multiple Rent Board petitions filed against properties.

name: Benny Chetcuti Jr.
notes: Ellis Acted at least 48 units in SF. Convicted of fraud — Ponzi scheme, $28M+ aggregate losses across 114 victims. State Desist & Refrain Order (2011). Forged documents. Under legal restrictions.

name: Thomas Aquilina
notes: At least 8 properties (6 units at 3150-3160 26th Street). Colluded with property manager German Maldonado to evict 20+ tenants. Rent theft by manager. Served informal eviction notices without legal process.

name: David Bellings
notes: 20 properties in SF. Listed as serial evictor by Anti-Eviction Mapping Project.

name: Paul and Peter Berger
notes: Multiple properties. Evicted 13 units in SF. Listed by Anti-Eviction Mapping Project.

name: Tenderloin Neighborhood Development Corp
notes: Non-profit. 25 communities, 3,351 units (2,734 low-cost), 22 fully affordable. Largest fully affordable housing provider on this list. Some controversy: laid off half staff (alleged union busting per SF Homeless Tenants Union).

name: Mercy Housing
notes: Non-profit, low-income focused. 34 communities in SF. Supportive services included.

name: Elba Borgen
notes: Serial evictor and speculator. Multiple properties.

name: Helene de Baubigny & John Golob
notes: Listed by Anti-Eviction Mapping Project. Multiple properties.

name: Brian & Sam Hunt
notes: Flipping and converting multi-unit buildings to luxury single-unit. Known properties: 306-308B Bartlett, 1964 Larkin.

name: Emily Benkert
notes: Short-term rental host with many listings. Multiple properties.

name: Behnam Halali
notes: Filed 8 eviction lawsuits against a single tenant (2022-2024), all dismissed. Multiple properties.

name: PAMA Management / Mike Nijjar
notes: CA Attorney General lawsuit filed June 2025 for habitability violations and deceptive leasing. Primarily South Bay/Hunters Point properties.
